name: normwear
paper_title: Toward Foundation Model for Multivariate Wearable Sensing of Physiological Signals
authors: Yunfei Luo, Yuliang Chen, Asif Salekin, Tauhidur Rahman
venue: ACM Transactions on Computing for Healthcare (2025). Preprint: arXiv:2412.09758 (Dec 2024, rev. May 2025)
weights_url: GitHub release v1.0.0-alpha: normwear_pretrain_ckpt.pth (backbone), normwear_msitf_zeroshot_last_checkpoint-5.pth (MSiTF aggregator). Text head: muzammil-eds/tinyllama-2.5T-Clinical-v2 (HuggingFace, ~1.1B).
role: baseline (bespoke MSiTF / L1 text-matching tier) — the released open-vocab wearable foundation model whose CWT-scalogram tokenizer most directly contests HALO's physical-Hz filterbank; must-have head-to-head.
notes: First multimodal wearable-sensing foundation model. Channel-INDEPENDENT ViT over ricker continuous-wavelet-transform (CWT) scalograms per channel; query-conditioned MSiTF aggregator fuses per-channel patch tokens into one 2048-d vector aligned to a frozen Clinical-TinyLlama (~1.1B) text head. Pretrained on ~2.5M multivariate segments / 14,943 h of PPG/ECG/EEG/GSR/IMU. Zero-shot HAR = MINIMUM MANHATTAN (L1) distance between the signal embedding and each candidate label's TinyLlama embedding (NOT cosine: sensor/text spaces are asymmetric, native metric is L1). Adapter contract: 6 real acc+gyro channels (no phantom/zero-pad), resample to 65 Hz (ricker scales are 65 Hz-tuned; get_embedding only resamples internally above 256 Hz), 6 s (390-sample) windows, per-channel linear de-trend (removes gravity DC) + amplitude-normalize by mean|x|, unit-agnostic. Ported from the legacy 'l1 tier' adapter; the 65 Hz resample (offline in legacy preprocess_normwear.py) is folded per-window into the adapter here. Reuses the on-disk NormWear repo code + released checkpoints; vendored repo/ copy is a follow-up.
